title: MARA and EDF Pulse Ventures Sign Investment Agreement in Subsidiary Exaion to Expand MARA's Global AI/HPC Capabilities
text: Marathon Digital Holdings announced an investment agreement allowing MARA to acquire a 64% stake in Exaion, a subsidiary of EDF, one of the world's largest low-carbon energy producers. MARA would invest approximately $168 million in cash upfront to acquire the 64% stake in Exaion, with the opportunity to invest another approximately $127 million to acquire an additional 11% of Exaion contingent upon certain milestones. The transaction is expected to close in or around Q4 2025 subject to satisfying conditions precedent and regulatory approvals. Exaion develops and operates high-performance computing data centers and provides secure cloud and AI infrastructure in partnership with NVIDIA, Deloitte, and 2CRSI. Exaion's existing infrastructure includes four data centers and 1,250 GPUs. This reflects MARA's strategic diversification into AI and HPC technologies, gaining access to established European enterprise and government clients.
